name: What proof do I need they copied my New York Times-style article?
description: Screenshots of matching outputs, queries, timestamps.[2][3]

mainEntity.name: What proof do I need they copied my New York Times-style article?

acceptedAnswer.text: Screenshots of matching outputs, queries, timestamps.[2][3]
acceptedAnswer.description: Screenshots of Perplexity outputs matching your article verbatim/substantially, query logs, and metadata showing scraping.[2][3] Context: Complaints detail PerplexityBot access, even paywalled content.[1][2] Key factors: Side-by-side comparisons, timestamps, no transformation (outputs substitute original).[3] Use Wayback Machine for archives. In NY cases, courts credit such evidence.[4] Preserve server logs if available. This evidence beats fair use.
